stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking activity around a significant LLM model.

stories.title: Sony is killing all physical PlayStation game discs
stories.author: Dominic Preston
bullet_points: Sony is phasing out physical game discs for new PlayStation titles.
The complete transition to digital-only releases is scheduled to begin in January 2028.
Games released prior to the cutoff date will remain available in physical disc format.

stories.title: Apple takes Epic fight over app store fees to the Supreme Court
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Apple is escalating its legal battle with Epic Games to the Supreme Court.
The core issue before the Supreme Court is whether a previous contempt finding against Apple in the Epic case was legally erroneous.
This case has major implications for app store fee structures and platform regulation in the tech industry.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1 model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in generative AI tasks.
The model is categorized under major AI frameworks like transformers and utilizes safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-V4-Pro model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a large language model.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, suggesting integration into existing AI workflows.

stories.title: Faster Gemma 4 on MLX with multi-token prediction
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Gemma 4 inference speed is significantly improved in Ollama 0.31 on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is achieved through Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) powered by MLX.
Coding agent performance saw up to a 90% speed increase when benchmarked with Aider.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using the MLX engine.
The performance improvements translate to higher quality model responses.
The updated engine also enables faster response times and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released as an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents run locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance gains.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing performance on Apple hardware.
This is presented as a preview, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging apps with local AI coding agents.
The core feature emphasizes running AI entirely on the user's own device for privacy/control.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: New attack provides one more reason why AI browsers are a bad idea
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A new vulnerability demonstrates the susceptibility of Large Language Models (LLMs) to prompt injection.
The attack vector involves deceptively simple inputs, such as instructing an LLM that '2 + 2 = 5', to bypass safety guardrails.
The article argues that this type of vulnerability undermines the viability and safety of AI-integrated browsers.

stories.title: Reinforcement Learning with Metacognitive Feedback Elicits Faithful Uncertainty Expression in LLMs
stories.author: Arman Cohan
bullet_points: The research addresses a critical weakness in LLMs: the inability to accurately express uncertainty and avoid confident hallucinations.
It proposes using Reinforcement Learning augmented with Metacognitive Feedback to improve LLM self-monitoring capabilities.
This work aims to enhance the trustworthiness and reliability of Large Language Models by giving them better awareness of their own knowledge boundaries.

stories.title: AxDafny: Agentic Verified Code Generation in Dafny
stories.author: Leopoldo Sarra
bullet_points: Introduces AxDafny, a framework for agentic code generation in Dafny that simultaneously generates executable code and formal verification proofs.
The system uses a verifier-guided repair mechanism to iteratively refine implementations, invariants, assertions, and termination arguments.
Presents LiveCodeBench-Pro-Dafny (LCB-Pro-Dafny), a new benchmark featuring 250 competition-style problems with formal specifications for evaluation.

stories.title: Surrogate Fidelity: When Can Open LLMs Explain Closed Ones?
stories.author: Nehal Bandi
bullet_points: The paper addresses the 'surrogate problem' in mechanistic interpretability (MI) when analyzing closed, proprietary LLMs.
It investigates the reliability of using measurements from accessible, open LLMs to infer behavior in inaccessible, closed models.
Fidelity is evaluated across prediction, attribution, and representation levels, suggesting methods for API-compatible analysis.

stories.title: Reinforcement Learning with Metacognitive Feedback Elicits Faithful Uncertainty Expression in LLMs
stories.author: Arman Cohan
bullet_points: The research addresses a critical weakness in Large Language Models (LLMs): their inability to accurately express uncertainty and avoid confident hallucinations.
The proposed solution involves integrating Reinforcement Learning with Metacognitive Feedback to improve self-monitoring capabilities in LLMs.
This work directly targets the trustworthiness and reliability of LLMs by enhancing their ability to recognize knowledge boundaries.

stories.title: DigitalCoach: Communication and Grounding Gaps in Human and Agentic Computer Use Coaching
stories.author: Amy Pavel
bullet_points: Introduces 'DigitalCoach,' a novel multimodal dataset for evaluating AI coaching in human-computer interaction.
The dataset comprises 72 expert-novice coaching sessions, including screen and input event recordings across five software applications.
The research addresses the critical gap of whether advanced AI agents can effectively teach humans software usage.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release introduces improved performance and expanded GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to enhance model support across diverse hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine specifically for better performance on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Introduction of NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for subagents within Claude Code.
The update also enables web search functionality within the Claude Code environment.
This suggests an enhancement in the capabilities of local LLM orchestration tools for complex tasks.

stories.title: Sony is closing the PS3 and Vita digital stores
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: Sony is shutting down the digital distribution services for the PS3 and PS Vita.
The PS3 digital store closure will begin in select Latin American markets, such as Mexico, Honduras, and Nicaragua, starting in August.
This marks the end of an era for digital content availability on these older PlayStation consoles.

stories.title: Google built a great smart speaker, but Gemini isn’t ready for it
stories.author: Jennifer Pattison Tuohy
bullet_points: Smart speakers are struggling to find a compelling use case beyond basic functions.
AI integration, specifically with Google's Gemini, is expected to be the next major evolution for the category.
The article suggests Google has developed capable hardware, but the current Gemini integration is not yet fully realized or ready for the device.

stories.title: Cleared by the US, derailed by the UK: Getty’s Shutterstock merger falls apart
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: Getty is abandoning its $3.7 billion merger with Shutterstock.
The deal failed due to restrictions imposed by a UK regulator.
The US Department of Justice had previously given the merger unconditional antitrust clearance.

stories.title: Meta is adding ridiculous ‘rate limits’ and a soft paywall to its smart glasses
stories.author: Sean Hollister
bullet_points: Meta is introducing usage limitations (rate limits) on its smart glasses' Conversation Focus feature.
Access to the feature will require a $19.99 Meta One Premium subscription after a certain usage threshold.
This move signals a shift towards monetizing AI hardware features within Meta's ecosystem.

stories.title: Reddit will require you to log in to use old.reddit.com
stories.author: Scharon Harding
bullet_points: Reddit is implementing a mandatory login requirement for accessing the legacy old.reddit.com interface.
The primary driver for this change is to combat abusive scraping activities.
This move represents a shift in Reddit's access control policies for older site versions.

stories.title: Amazon blames piracy apps with malware for killing new Fire Stick sideloading
stories.author: Scharon Harding
bullet_points: Amazon is addressing issues with third-party apps on Fire Sticks, specifically malware associated with piracy apps.
The new Fire Stick OS update includes features to block unauthorized third-party homepage launchers and ad blockers.
This move represents Amazon tightening control over the Fire Stick ecosystem to improve security and user experience.

stories.title: NASA may send a backup, nuclear-powered Mars rover to the Moon
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: NASA is considering sending a backup, nuclear-powered Mars rover to the Moon.
This concept suggests leveraging advanced, high-power propulsion/power sources for lunar missions.
The potential capability is described as 'awesome,' indicating high strategic interest.

stories.title: Google kills Tenor GIF API, forcing changes at X, Discord, and more
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google has discontinued its Tenor GIF API, impacting integrations across various platforms.
Platforms like X and Discord are now compelled to find alternative sources for GIF functionality.
Tenor's connection to Google applications remains functional, but external API access has been restricted.

stories.title: Introspective Coupling: Self-Explanation Training Tracks Behavioral Change Despite Fixed Supervision
stories.author: Belinda Z. Li
bullet_points: The research investigates how to ensure language models (LMs) provide 'faithful introspection' rather than just superficial explanations.
The study uses counterfactual behavior on modified inputs as a form of supervision for training explanation-generating LMs.
Results suggest that training LMs on fixed counterfactual explanations from past checkpoints or similar models can still drive behavioral change.

stories.title: QVal: Cheaply Evaluating Dense Supervision Signals for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
stories.author: Matthias Bethge
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of sparse rewards in long-horizon LLM agent tasks.
Proposes QVal, a method for cheaply evaluating dense supervision signals for LLM agents.
Highlights the common issue where dense supervision methods are evaluated only by downstream performance.

stories.title: When LLMs Read Tables Carelessly: Measuring and Reducing Data Referencing Errors
stories.author: Huzefa Rangwala
bullet_points: LLMs exhibit Data Referencing Errors (DREs) when processing tables, even when understanding the table structure.
DREs undermine the reliability of intermediate reasoning steps, not just the final answer.
This work provides the first systematic, large-scale evaluation of these errors in tabular data processing.

stories.title: Freeform Preference Learning for Robotic Manipulation
stories.author: Chelsea Finn
bullet_points: Addresses the bottleneck of reward design in autonomous robotic manipulation, particularly for long-horizon tasks.
Introduces Freeform Preference Learning (FPL) to overcome the limitations of binary preference labeling.
Allows human annotators to provide nuanced feedback on robot trajectories rather than just choosing a single 'better' option.

stories.title: AdaJEPA: An Adaptive Latent World Model
stories.author: Mengye Ren
bullet_points: Introduces AdaJEPA, an adaptive latent world model designed to overcome limitations in traditional latent world models.
The core innovation is performing test-time adaptation within the closed-loop of Model Predictive Control (MPC).
This addresses the critical issue of planning failure due to prediction inaccuracies or test-time distribution shift.

stories.title: TRIAGE: Role-Typed Credit Assignment for Agentic Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Alborz Geramifard
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in standard Reinforcement Learning (RL) for agentic systems: the uniform credit assignment based on final outcomes.
TRIAGE proposes a 'role-typed credit assignment' framework to better attribute credit to specific environment interactions (searches, clicks, edits, etc.).
This framework aims to solve issues where standard methods incorrectly penalize useful exploration or reinforce redundant actions.

stories.title: PolicyGuard: From Organizational Policies to Neuro-SymbolicCompliance Review Engines
stories.author: Amar Prakash Azad
bullet_points: PolicyGuard introduces a neuro-symbolic framework to address limitations in LLM-based compliance review.
It solves the problem of implicit policy logic in end-to-end LLM prompting, making compliance decisions opaque.
The system allows for explicit, inspectable, and testable application of organization-specific policies to documents.

stories.title: Introspective Coupling: Self-Explanation Training Tracks Behavioral Change Despite Fixed Supervision
stories.author: Belinda Z. Li
bullet_points: The research investigates whether training Language Models (LMs) to generate explanations leads to genuine 'faithful introspection' or just superficial imitation.
The study uses counterfactual behavior on modified inputs as a form of supervision for the LMs' self-explanation training.
Key findings suggest that LMs can be trained effectively using fixed counterfactual explanations derived from past checkpoints or similar models.

stories.title: QVal: Cheaply Evaluating Dense Supervision Signals for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
stories.author: Matthias Bethge
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of sparse rewards in long-horizon LLM agent tasks.
Proposes QVal, a method for cheaply evaluating dense supervision signals for intermediate actions.
Focuses on improving guidance for LLMs by moving beyond sole reliance on final outcome rewards.

stories.title: Freeform Preference Learning for Robotic Manipulation
stories.author: Chelsea Finn
bullet_points: Addresses the bottleneck of reward design in autonomous robot policy improvement, particularly for long-horizon tasks.
Introduces Freeform Preference Learning (FPL) to learn robot policies directly from human preferences.
FPL overcomes the limitations of binary preference comparisons by allowing annotators to define preferences in a more natural, freeform manner.

stories.title: AdaJEPA: An Adaptive Latent World Model
stories.author: Mengye Ren
bullet_points: Introduces AdaJEPA, an adaptive latent world model designed to improve planning capabilities.
Addresses the limitation of traditional latent world models being frozen at test time, which causes planning failure under distribution shift.
Integrates test-time adaptation directly within the closed loop of Model Predictive Control (MPC).

stories.title: Automated Background Swapping for Robustness against Spurious Backgrounds
stories.author: Kajetan Schweighofer
bullet_points: The research addresses the critical issue of spurious correlations in Deep Neural Networks, where models rely on non-causal features for prediction.
Specifically targets the vision domain, focusing on how background elements can introduce misleading correlations that cause catastrophic failure during generalization.
Proposes an 'Automated Background Swapping' technique to enhance model robustness against these spurious background dependencies.

stories.title: TRIAGE: Role-Typed Credit Assignment for Agentic Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Alborz Geramifard
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in standard Reinforcement Learning (GRPO) when applying it to agentic systems.
It introduces TRIAGE, a novel role-typed credit assignment framework for better action evaluation.
TRIAGE aims to solve the issue where final outcomes unfairly penalize useful exploration or reinforce redundant actions.

stories.title: FedLAB: Traceable Semantic Codebooks for Federated Multimodal Graph Foundation Learning
stories.author: Guoren Wang
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of training multimodal graph foundation models when data is decentralized across private clients.
It introduces 'FedLAB,' a method designed for federated multimodal graph foundation learning to preserve privacy.
The goal is to enable the learning of reusable knowledge from complex graphs enriched with various modalities (text, images, etc.) without central data sharing.

stories.title: CoMet: Context and Multiplicity Decomposition for Multimodal Uncertainty Estimation
stories.author: Olga Russakovsky
bullet_points: The paper addresses the critical and difficult problem of uncertainty estimation in AI models, particularly Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
It introduces 'CoMet,' a method designed for Context and Multiplicity Decomposition to better estimate this uncertainty.
The research highlights that uncertainty in MLLMs can arise from various sources and the complex relationships between them.

stories.title: Introspective Coupling: Self-Explanation Training Tracks Behavioral Change Despite Fixed Supervision
stories.author: Belinda Z. Li
bullet_points: The research investigates whether training Language Models (LMs) to generate explanations leads to genuine 'faithful introspection' or just superficial imitation.
The study uses counterfactual behavior on modified inputs as a novel form of supervision for training these self-explaining LMs.
Findings suggest that LMs can be trained effectively using fixed counterfactual explanations derived from past checkpoints or similar models.

stories.title: QVal: Cheaply Evaluating Dense Supervision Signals for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
stories.author: Matthias Bethge
bullet_points: The paper addresses the challenge of sparse rewards in long-horizon LLM agent tasks.
It focuses on 'QVal,' a method for cheaply evaluating dense supervision signals at intermediate steps.
The research aims to improve LLM agent training by providing better guidance than just final outcome rewards.

stories.title: When LLMs Read Tables Carelessly: Measuring and Reducing Data Referencing Errors
stories.author: Huzefa Rangwala
bullet_points: LLMs exhibit Data Referencing Errors (DREs) when processing tables, even when understanding the table structure.
DREs are critical because they undermine the correctness of intermediate reasoning steps, not just the final answer.
This research provides the first systematic evaluation of these errors across various contexts.

stories.title: Generative Skill Composition for LLM Agents
stories.author: Tianlong Chen
bullet_points: The paper addresses the bottleneck of selecting appropriate skill compositions for LLM agents, which use modular 'skills' to solve complex tasks.
Skills are defined as reusable packages of procedural knowledge for specialized actions (e.g., environment setup, testing, refactoring).
The research contrasts existing approaches to skill composition selection, suggesting a need for better methods as skill libraries expand.

stories.title: Scalable Behaviour Cloning on Browser Using via Skill Distillation
stories.author: Qinhuai Na
bullet_points: Proposes using human web browsing behavior as a scalable source of reusable skills for AI agents.
Argues that the primary challenge for browser agents is decision-making under incomplete information, not low-level operations.
Introduces 'Skill Distillation' to leverage implicit knowledge present in human interaction traces.

stories.title: MECoBench: A Systematic Study of Multimodal Agent Collaboration in Embodied Environments
stories.author: Zhongyu Wei
bullet_points: Introduces MECoBench, a new benchmark for evaluating multimodal agent collaboration in embodied environments.
Addresses the gap in understanding how multimodal LLMs can effectively collaborate in visually grounded tasks.
The benchmark tests various MLLMs across diverse real-world tasks, cooperation structures, and collaboration modes.

stories.title: Single Dose of Frog-Derived Gut Bacterium Eradicates 100% of Tumors in Mice
stories.author: mpweiher
bullet_points: Research suggests a single dose of frog-derived gut bacteria can eliminate tumors in mice.
This represents a significant finding in the field of microbiome-based cancer therapy.
The study originates from a platform like Hacker News, indicating scientific/bio-tech interest.

stories.title: Claude Code is steganographically marking requests
stories.author: kirushik
bullet_points: Claude AI is reportedly using steganography to mark user requests.
This suggests a hidden mechanism or metadata embedding within API calls.
The topic raises questions about data privacy and model introspection.

stories.title: How Jujutsu Rethinks Git's Working Copy and Conflict Model
stories.author: git-tower.com by brunobrito
bullet_points: Jujutsu is a new version control system aiming to fundamentally rethink Git's architecture.
It introduces a novel approach to managing the working copy and handling merge conflicts.
This represents an interesting, though niche, technical discussion within the developer tooling space.
